sections.title: Extracurriculars & Stats
sections.blurb: Your activities, scores, and academic standing.

sections.title: Interests
sections.blurb: What genuinely pulls you in — subjects, problems, and passions.

sections.blurb: How you work, live, and thrive day to day.

questions.text: What's your SAT total score? (Leave blank if you took the ACT instead or haven't tested yet.)
What's your ACT composite score? (Leave blank if you used the SAT or haven't tested yet.)
Which extracurricular takes up most of your time — and how deep does it go?

options.label: STEM (robotics, coding, research, olympiads) with leadership or awards
Arts or publications (band, theater, writing, film) with serious commitment
Leadership, debate, or service (student government, volunteering)
I spread time across several activities without one dominant focus

questions.text: It's a free Saturday afternoon. Which sounds most fun?

options.label: Building an app, gadget, or experiment
Sketching, writing, filming, or making music
Reading about people, history, or current events
Exploring nature, health, or how living systems work

questions.text: What kind of problem is most satisfying to solve?

options.label: One with a clear, correct answer I can prove
One where I build something people actually use
One where I change someone's mind or behavior
One with no single right answer — interpretation matters

questions.text: Twenty years from now, which achievement would make you proudest?

options.label: A discovery or invention that pushed knowledge forward
A company or product used by millions
Art, writing, or performances that moved people
Policy or advocacy that made society fairer or healthier

questions.text: How do you prefer to work on hard problems?

options.label: Deep solo focus — headphones on, full immersion
Pairing or small teams where we bounce ideas quickly
Discussion-heavy seminars and debates
Hands-on studios, labs, or workshops

questions.text: Which campus vibe fits you best?

options.label: Intense and ambitious — everyone is grinding toward something big
Collaborative and balanced — strong academics without constant pressure
Creative and open-ended — room to explore across fields
Close-knit and residential — I want to know my classmates well
Small and tight-knit (under 5,000 undergrads)
Big and buzzing (15,000+)
Size doesn't matter much to me

questions.text: Which matters most in your ideal program?

options.label: Flexibility to combine fields and design my own path
Depth — world-class training in one discipline
Career outcomes and professional networks
Hands-on making, performing, or research from day one
